product_name: CIBIL Score — Guide for Union Bank Staff

score_ranges.loan_impact: Best rates, fast approval, higher loan amounts
Application likely rejected at most banks
First-time borrower — need to build credit

what_affects_score.detail: Whether EMIs and credit card bills are being paid on time
Percentage of credit card limit being used — keep it below 30%
Older accounts are better — longer history is advantageous
A mix of secured + unsecured loans is considered positive
Applying for loans at multiple places simultaneously can lower the score

improvement_tips: Pay all EMIs and credit card bills before the due date — impact shows within 1 month
Keep credit card outstanding below 30% of the total limit
Do not close old credit card accounts — it reduces credit history length
Avoid multiple loan applications simultaneously — each inquiry lowers the score
Consistent good behavior for 6-12 months can improve the score by 50-100 points
Taking a secured loan (gold loan / loan against FD) helps start credit history (for those with no history)

how_to_check.detail: CIBIL check facility available in Union Mobile app
Free unlimited checks (soft inquiry — no impact on score)

soft_vs_hard_inquiry.soft: When a customer checks their own score — no impact on score
soft_vs_hard_inquiry.hard: When a bank pulls CIBIL for a loan application — score drops slightly temporarily

staff_note: Written or digital consent from the customer is mandatory before pulling their CIBIL report — RBI guidelines
